## Context

The 3 notification edge fns (\`notification-dispatcher\`, \`sms-scheduler\`, \`twilio-webhook\`) ship to DEV but are blocked on A2P 10DLC SMS registration (#127 LLC/EIN chain) before they handle production traffic. Session 60 / #371 (PR #441) established the Vitest harness pattern but kept this stack out of scope since it can't be exercised in PROD until #127 clears.

## Goal

Apply the Vitest harness pattern to all 3 notification edge fns so they have automated coverage when A2P 10DLC clears.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Each fn split into \`handler.ts\` + thin \`index.ts\` wrapper
- [ ] \`notification-dispatcher/handler.test.ts\` covers: in-app channel dispatch, email channel via Resend, SMS channel via Twilio (mocked), 5 SMS guards (TCPA opt-in, suppression list, quiet hours, daily cap, test mode), per-channel preference resolution
- [ ] \`sms-scheduler/handler.test.ts\` covers: daily cron dispatch, admin override mode, destination filter (skips \`destination IS NULL\`)
- [ ] \`twilio-webhook/handler.test.ts\` covers: delivery status update, STOP opt-out (TCPA compliance — adds row to \`sms_suppression_log\`), signature validation
- [ ] Reuses \`createEdgeFnSupabaseMock\`; may need a small \`twilio-mock.ts\` factory in \`_shared/__tests__/\`
- [ ] \`@p0\` tags on the SMS guard chain (highest risk) and STOP opt-out (compliance-critical)

## Estimated effort

~6-8h (3 fns, each with multiple branches; new Twilio mock infra).

## Related

- #371 — harness pattern
- #127 — A2P 10DLC SMS unblocker
title: Add edge-fn tests for notification stack (notification-dispatcher, sms-scheduler, twilio-webhook)

Phase 22 (Session 57) shipped the \`ingest-support-docs\` edge fn that syncs \`docs/support/**.md\` → \`support_docs\` table. It was untested at ship time. Session 60 / #371 (PR #441) established the Vitest harness pattern but kept this fn out of scope (admin-only ETL, lower risk surface).

## Goal

Apply the Vitest harness pattern to \`supabase/functions/ingest-support-docs/index.ts\`.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Split into \`handler.ts\` + thin \`index.ts\` wrapper
- [ ] Tests cover: admin auth check, frontmatter parsing happy path, frontmatter validation errors (missing required fields), legal-blocked draft handling (\`status: draft\`), upsert into \`support_docs\` table
- [ ] Reuses \`createEdgeFnSupabaseMock\`
- [ ] At least one \`@p0\` on the upsert happy path

comments.body:
## Implementation guide

Following DEC-037 / the pattern established in #371 (PR #441) + extended in #442 (PR #446).

### Code map (already in place)

\`supabase/functions/ingest-support-docs/index.ts\` is a 336-line file with **good internal structure** — the heavy lifting is in pure functions that can be tested directly without a handler split:

- \`slugFromPath(path)\` — derives \`<folder>/<name>\` slug from \`docs/support/<folder>/<name>.md\`; returns null for top-level meta docs
- \`parseSections(body)\` — extracts \`## Summary | Details | Examples | Related\` sections into a keyed object
- \`validateFrontmatter(fm, path)\` — returns array of error strings; covers required fields, valid doc_type, valid status, audience-must-be-array, legal_review_required + active gate
- \`parseDoc(file)\` — combines the three above; returns ParsedDoc or null
- \`handler(req)\` — the Deno.serve entry — auth + JSON parse + loop + upsert + delete

### Refactor scope

**Two-step refactor:**

1. **Export the pure functions from a new `parsers.ts` file** so they can be Vitest-tested without any of the URL-import or env-var concerns:
   \`\`\`
   ingest-support-docs/
     index.ts         <- 5-15 line Deno.serve wrapper
     handler.ts       <- handler(req, deps) — auth + payload validation + Supabase upsert/delete + error aggregation
     parsers.ts       <- exports slugFromPath, parseSections, validateFrontmatter, parseDoc
     parsers.test.ts  <- pure-function tests (no mocks needed)
     handler.test.ts  <- handler integration tests with mocked Supabase + INGEST_SUPPORT_DOCS_SECRET
   \`\`\`

2. **`handler.ts` deps shape:**
   \`\`\`ts
   export interface Deps {
     supabase: SupabaseLike;
     env: Record<string, string | undefined>;  // only INGEST_SUPPORT_DOCS_SECRET, SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY
   }
   \`\`\`
   No Stripe, no Resend, no fetch. Simpler than the Stripe-touching fns.

### Test plan

#### parsers.test.ts (~12 tests, all pure)

- **slugFromPath**
  - returns \`<folder>/<name>\` for valid path
  - returns null for top-level meta (\`docs/support/README.md\`, \`docs/support/GAP-ANALYSIS.md\`)
  - returns null for non-md files
- **parseSections**
  - extracts all 4 standard sections when present
  - returns empty object for body with no \`##\` headings
  - is case-insensitive on heading match
  - ignores non-standard \`## Foo\` headings (only \`summary | details | examples | related\`)
- **validateFrontmatter** (cover the gate logic)
  - happy path: all required fields present, valid types
  - missing field: each of the 6 required fields produces a specific error
  - invalid doc_type rejected (valid: policy / faq / process / guide)
  - invalid status rejected (valid: active / draft / archived)
  - audience non-array rejected
  - **the legal-review gate**: legal_review_required=true + status=active + missing reviewed_by/date → error \`@p0\`
  - legal_review_required=true + status=draft → no error (draft can wait for review)
- **parseDoc** integration
  - successful parse returns ParsedDoc with all fields
  - frontmatter errors propagate to ParsedDoc.errors
  - top-level meta path returns null

#### handler.test.ts (~8 tests)

Mock Supabase via \`createEdgeFnSupabaseMock\` (ignore the rate-limit helper — this fn doesn't use it). Mock the secret via \`env.INGEST_SUPPORT_DOCS_SECRET = \"test-secret\"\`.

- **method + auth guards** \`@p0\`
  - non-POST → 405
  - missing INGEST_SUPPORT_DOCS_SECRET in env → 500
  - missing or wrong Bearer token → 401
- **payload validation**
  - invalid JSON body → 400
  - missing files array → 400
  - empty files array + valid auth → 200 with \`ingested: 0, deleted: 0\`
- **happy path** \`@p0\`
  - 3 valid files → returns \`ingested: 3, errors: []\`; verify \`supabase.from(\"support_docs\").upsert(rows, { onConflict: \"slug\" })\` called with normalized row shape
- **partial failure**
  - 2 valid + 1 with frontmatter error → \`ingested: 2, errors: [\"<path>: missing required field ...\"]\`
- **draft handling**
  - file with \`status: draft\` and \`legal_review_required: true\` (no reviewed_by) → upserted successfully (no validation error because not active)
- **delete cascade**
  - happy upsert → also calls \`supabase.from(\"support_docs\").delete().not(\"source_path\", \"in\", ...)\` to remove rows for files no longer in payload

### Estimated effort

~2-3h. The pure-functions split is the bulk of the value — those tests are trivial since there's nothing to mock. The handler test is straightforward because there's no Stripe/Resend/external fetch.

### Acceptance criteria checklist

- [ ] Refactored into \`parsers.ts\` + \`handler.ts\` + 5-line \`index.ts\` Deno wrapper
- [ ] \`parsers.test.ts\` covers slugFromPath, parseSections, validateFrontmatter, parseDoc (~12 tests)
- [ ] \`handler.test.ts\` covers auth, payload validation, happy path, partial failure, draft handling, delete cascade (~8 tests)
- [ ] At least one \`@p0\` on the legal-review gate test (compliance-critical) and one on the upsert happy path
- [ ] \`npm run test\` + \`npm run docs:sync-check\` + \`npm run build\` all green
- [ ] TESTING-STATUS bumped (~+20 tests)

### What I'd consider while implementing

- The \`delete\` query uses a hand-built \`in\` filter string. The mock chain may not differentiate this from a normal \`delete\`, so the test asserts via spy on \`supabase.from(\"support_docs\").delete\` invocation count, not on the filter shape.
- \`gray-matter\` is the markdown frontmatter parser — keep importing it from \`npm:gray-matter@4.0.3\` in the parsers.ts file. This works in Vitest because npm: prefix is recognized at install time. (Caveat: confirm before assuming. If it breaks Vitest resolver, mock \`gray-matter\` via \`vi.mock()\` in the test.)

title: Add edge-fn test for ingest-support-docs (admin ETL)

## Context

`docs/PROJECT-HUB.md` Session Handoff section currently holds Sessions 25-59 (~34 entries). Per `CLAUDE.md` Phase 6 doc-update checklist:

> `docs/COMPLETED-PHASES.md` — only at session close, for sessions aging out of PROJECT-HUB. Move session-handoff entries older than ~5 sessions from PROJECT-HUB to this archive. Keep PROJECT-HUB Session Handoff section lean (current + last 5 sessions).

This means Sessions **25-54** should be archived out of PROJECT-HUB, leaving Sessions 55-59 inline.

Surfaced by Session 60 doc audit. The `docs:sync-check` doesn't track this drift directly, but PROJECT-HUB has bloated to the point that the Session Handoff section dominates the file.

## Goal

Migrate Session 25-54 handoff entries verbatim from `PROJECT-HUB.md` to `COMPLETED-PHASES.md`, preserving structure and headings. PROJECT-HUB retains current session + last 5 (currently 55-59).

## Scope

1. Identify the boundary in PROJECT-HUB ('Session 54' last entry to archive; 'Session 55' first entry to keep)
2. Append entries 25-54 to COMPLETED-PHASES.md (chronologically — newest entries already on top of COMPLETED-PHASES, so insert in reverse-chronological order matching the existing convention)
3. Delete the migrated entries from PROJECT-HUB Session Handoff section
4. Update the section header in PROJECT-HUB to reflect new range (e.g. 'Sessions 55-NN')
5. Update `change_type` frontmatter on both files to the session this archival is performed in
6. Bump body 'Last Updated' line in PROJECT-HUB

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `wc -l docs/PROJECT-HUB.md` significantly reduced (likely 60% smaller)
- [ ] Every session-handoff entry that was in PROJECT-HUB pre-archive now exists in exactly one place (PROJECT-HUB if 55+, COMPLETED-PHASES if ≤54)
- [ ] No content rewritten — verbatim move
- [ ] `npm run docs:sync-check` still passes
- [ ] Build clean

## Out of scope

- Restructuring or summarising session entries
- Updating the entries themselves

## Why deferred

Session 60 doc audit favored 'stamp + defer' over inline archival to keep the Session 60 PR focused on #371 (edge-fn test harness). This is mechanical reorganization with a large diff — best done in a dedicated PR.
title: Archive PROJECT-HUB session handoffs 25-54 to COMPLETED-PHASES

## Context

RAV is pre-incorporation (#127 blocks Stripe Tax, bank, Puzzle.io accounting, legal ToS, A2P 10DLC SMS). Before / during the LLC formation pass, the founding team needs a starter library of legal-and-operational documents: operating agreement, founder understanding, equity-if-applicable language, customer terms templates.

Two practical sources:
- **Stripe Atlas** (and similar: Clerky, LegalZoom, Firstbase) publishes high-quality templates that most early-stage companies start from — often free samples that become the basis for a lawyer review rather than a clean-sheet draft.
- **Our own `docs/support/policies/`** already has privacy-policy, booking-terms, payment-policy, trust-safety-policy, insurance-liability-policy, subscription-terms drafts sitting at `status: draft` pending #80 lawyer review. These are platform-terms, not incorporation-docs — but same lawyer pass can cover both.

## Goal

Build a `docs/incorporation/` folder containing:

- **`OPERATING-AGREEMENT.md`** — single-member LLC template as starting point. Stripe Atlas samples are a common floor; we adapt for RAV's ownership structure.
- **`FOUNDER-UNDERSTANDING.md`** — if there are multiple founders, a plain-language memo of who owns what, vesting, IP assignment, decision rights.
- **`FORMATION-CHECKLIST.md`** — state, registered agent, EIN, bank account, state tax registration, Stripe Atlas vs manual, cost comparison. Tracks what's done / pending.
- **`CUSTOMER-TERMS-OUTLINE.md`** — index of what our public ToS needs to cover (references the 6 drafts already sitting in `docs/support/policies/`).
- **`IP-ASSIGNMENT.md`** — any contributors (current + future) assigning IP to the LLC. Template language.
- **`STATE-TAX-NOTES.md`** — rough-notes pass on sales-tax / income-tax registration per state RAV operates in.

## Scope

Research + draft work. Lawyer ultimately reviews (same pass as #80), but getting to that review meeting with enhanced drafts rather than blank templates saves billable hours and gives the founder leverage.

## Not in scope (for this ticket)

- Actually incorporating — that's #127
- Legal review — that's the lawyer's job
- Live tax registration — that's post-incorporation

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `docs/incorporation/` folder with the 6 documents above, each with proper frontmatter (status: draft)
- [ ] Each doc has a "Source" section citing where template language came from (Stripe Atlas / LegalZoom / generated / original)
- [ ] `FORMATION-CHECKLIST.md` reflects current reality (what's done, what's pending, cost estimates)
- [ ] Cross-linked from PROJECT-HUB + PLATFORM-INVENTORY §4 Governance
- [ ] Flagged in #80 lawyer-review scope so the ToS-draft lawyer pass picks these up in the same engagement

## Related

- #127 LLC/EIN formation (the actual incorporation — this is the docs prep)
- #80 Legal consult (covers both platform ToS + incorporation docs)
- #404 Legal-blocked public policy drafts (already sitting at `status: draft` pending #80)
- `docs/support/policies/` — 6 existing legal-blocked drafts

## Estimated scope
2-4h research + drafting. Pure docs, no code.
title: Incorporation documentation starter kit — operating agreement, formation docs, Stripe samples

body:
## Context

Sentry.io covers error tracking + performance when users hit issues, but it does not do **synthetic uptime monitoring** — i.e. proactively checking that critical user journeys still work when no one happens to be using the site. Before launch (web + mobile), we want a single dashboard to see the health of the platform 24/7.

## Decision: Checkly (public SaaS, free tier)

Evaluated options:

| Tool | Type | Verdict |
|------|------|---------|
| **Checkly** | Synthetic browser + API checks (Playwright-based) | ✅ **Chosen** |
| Better Stack (Better Uptime) | HTTP ping + status page | Possible later addition for public status page |
| UptimeRobot | HTTP ping | Redundant with Better Stack |
| Zabbix / Uptime Kuma (self-hosted) | ❌ Rejected — don't want homelab dependency for prod monitoring |

### Why Checkly

1. **Real user-flow coverage, not just HTTP 200 pings.** Our risk isn't "is Vercel down" — it's "did a deploy silently break Stripe checkout / bid submission / voice search." Playwright-based synthetic checks catch that; HTTP pings don't.
2. **Reuses existing Playwright muscle.** We already have E2E tests in `e2e/smoke/` — scripts can nearly port over.
3. **Free tier:** 10k API check runs/month + 1.5k browser check runs/month, multi-location, public status page.
4. **Covers mobile indirectly** — mobile app depends on the same Supabase edge functions, so API checks against those cover backend health for web + mobile in one place.

## Scope

### Phase 1 — Critical user journeys (3-5 browser checks)
Candidates to pick from (TBD which to prioritize):
- [ ] Sign in → land on dashboard (auth path)
- [ ] Browse rentals → open property detail (discovery path)
- [ ] Place a bid on a listing (core marketplace action)
- [ ] Checkout flow to Stripe (payment path — stop before actual charge)
- [ ] Voice search returns results (voice pipeline)
- [ ] Admin dashboard loads (staff path)

### Phase 2 — API/edge function checks
- [ ] `api-gateway` public endpoints (GET listings, GET resorts)
- [ ] `stripe-webhook` reachability
- [ ] `notification-dispatcher` reachability
- [ ] Supabase REST health (basic ping)

### Phase 3 — Alerting
- [ ] Route Checkly alerts to email (RAV team) + optionally Slack/webhook
- [ ] Define severity (all flows = P1, API = P2)
- [ ] On-call rotation decision (later)

### Phase 4 — Optional: public status page
- [ ] Decide if we want a customer-facing status page (status.rent-a-vacation.com)
- [ ] If yes, evaluate Checkly's built-in status page vs. Better Stack free tier

## Success criteria

- One dashboard shows health of all critical user journeys (web) + backend APIs (covers mobile too)
- Alert within 5 minutes of a critical-path regression
- Browser check run-count stays within free tier (1.5k runs/mo = ~3 flows at 10-min intervals)

## Not in scope

- APM / distributed tracing (Sentry already does this)
- Error tracking (Sentry)
- Log aggregation (separate concern — revisit if needed)
- Self-hosted monitoring (explicit decision against)

## References

- Checkly free tier: https://www.checklyhq.com/pricing
- Existing Playwright E2E tests: `e2e/smoke/`
- Sentry config: already deployed, `rent-a-vacation-org` / `rav-website`
title: Platform Monitoring: Synthetic uptime checks via Checkly (SaaS)

## Status
**DESIGN COMPLETE — Implementation deferred until after beta launch**

This feature is parked until we have 30+ days of real proposal data to validate the assumptions below. The design is locked; only the implementation waits.

## Spec
`Features/WS4-Proposal-Logic-Feature-Brief.md` (April 2026)

## Problem
Two related gaps in the current Vacation Wishes proposal flow:

**Gap 1 — No matching approved property:** Owner browses Vacation Wishes, sees a traveler wants Colorado, but only has Florida properties. Today they hit a dead end with no guidance.

**Gap 2 — Location enforcement not working:** Owner with only Florida properties can submit a proposal for a Colorado request. Nothing blocks this today.

## Locked Design Decisions

### Decision 1: Location matching logic
- Match at city level: `travel_requests.destination_location` ILIKE `%{property.city}%` OR reverse
- Reuse the same matching logic already in the `match-travel-requests` edge function
- UI blocks proposal before submission if no properties match
- DB check constraint or RLS as secondary guard (defense in depth)

### Decision 2: No proposals for unapproved properties (even "in review")
- Properties with `status != 'active'` cannot be proposed
- Pending-review properties show status clearly: "Your [Resort Name] property is under review"
- Do NOT let owners propose pending properties — a traveler accepting an unverified property is a trust-breaking scenario

### Decision 3: Option C — Fast-track list + propose path
- If owner has no approved property for destination, show "List this property" CTA
- CTA deep-links to `/list-property` with destination pre-filled
- Show deadline context: "This request expires in X days. Standard property review takes 24-48 hours."
- After approval, owner gets notification to propose

### Decision 4: Traveler-set destination is binding
- No alternative destinations; the city from the request is fixed
- Only exception: if `dates_flexible = true`, owner may propose different dates (already supported)

## What Needs To Be Built (when unblocked)

### Migration (next available number)
```sql
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_owner_matching_properties(
  p_owner_id UUID,
  p_destination TEXT
) RETURNS TABLE (
  property_id UUID, property_name TEXT, resort_name TEXT,
  city TEXT, brand TEXT, status TEXT
) AS $$
BEGIN
  RETURN QUERY
  SELECT p.id, p.name, p.resort_name, p.city, p.brand::TEXT, p.status::TEXT
  FROM public.properties p
  WHERE p.owner_id = p_owner_id
    AND p.status = 'active'
    AND (
      p.city ILIKE '%' || p_destination || '%'
      OR p_destination ILIKE '%' || p.city || '%'
    );
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER;
```

### Frontend
- New `src/components/bidding/ProposalSubmitButton.tsx` — smart component that checks matches before showing proposal form
- New `src/components/bidding/NoMatchProposalPrompt.tsx` — "List your property" prompt UI with expiry countdown
- `src/hooks/useBidding.ts` `useCreateProposal` — pre-submission location validation with clear error
- `src/pages/BiddingMarketplace.tsx` — wire the new smart button
- `src/flows/owner-lifecycle.ts` — add `no_match_proposal_prompt` step

### Backend
- `supabase/functions/match-travel-requests/index.ts` — add pending-owner notification logic: when a property is approved and matches open requests, fire `new_travel_request_match` notification
- DB-level defense: CHECK constraint or RLS on `travel_proposals` enforcing city match

## Acceptance Criteria (for implementation time)
- [ ] Owner with no matching approved property sees "List your property" prompt
- [ ] Owner with pending-review property sees "under review" message (not proposal form)
- [ ] Owner with approved matching property sees normal proposal form (no regression)
- [ ] Submitting a proposal for a non-matching location is blocked at both UI and hook level
- [ ] When owner's property is approved and matches open requests, notification fires
- [ ] "List My Property" CTA pre-fills destination from the request
- [ ] All existing proposal tests still pass

## When to unpark this
- Beta launch complete
- 30+ days of real proposal data collected
- Answer these questions first:
  - Do owners actually encounter "no matching property" frequently?
  - What % of proposals fail on location mismatch?
  - Is the 24-48hr fast-track review window feasible at beta scale?

labels.name: post-beta
labels.description: Deferred until after beta launch + real usage data

body:
# Audit Scraped Resort Data for Legal Compliance

## Context
During early development, public data from resort brand websites (Hilton, Marriott, Disney, etc.) was collected to seed the platform with resort/property information — names, locations, amenity descriptions, and potentially images. This data was used to populate the initial brand, vacation property, and location records.

**Before production launch, we must ensure this data usage is legally compliant.**

## Risk Assessment

| Data Type | Risk Level | Concern |
|-----------|-----------|---------|
| Resort names & addresses | **Low** | Facts are not copyrightable |
| Number of rooms, unit sizes | **Low** | Factual data |
| Amenity lists (pool, gym, etc.) | **Low** | Factual/generic |
| Resort descriptions (verbatim text) | **Medium-High** | May be copyrighted marketing copy |
| Photos from brand websites | **High** | Almost certainly copyrighted |
| Brand logos | **High** | Trademarked — fair use is narrow |
| Pricing / fee data | **Low-Medium** | Factual but may be terms-of-use restricted |

## Audit Tasks

### 1. Inventory what was scraped
- [ ] Identify all seed data sources (which URLs / sites were scraped)
- [ ] List all database tables that contain seeded data
- [ ] Categorize each field: factual data vs. creative content vs. images
- [ ] Document: is the seed data in DEV only, or also in PROD?

### 2. Check for copyright issues
- [ ] Review all resort/property descriptions — are any copied verbatim from brand websites?
- [ ] Identify any images sourced from brand websites (check Supabase Storage buckets)
- [ ] Check if any brand logos are stored/used beyond fair-use trademark mention
- [ ] Review the `seed-manager` edge function and seed data scripts for source attribution

### 3. Remediation plan
- [ ] **Descriptions:** Rewrite any verbatim-copied text in original language. Can reference facts (\"oceanfront, 2 pools, golf course\") but not copy marketing prose.
- [ ] **Images:** Replace brand-sourced images with:
  - Owner-uploaded photos (production model)
  - Licensed stock photography (Unsplash, Pexels — free for commercial use)
  - Placeholder images with \"Owner photo coming soon\"
- [ ] **Brand mentions:** Using brand names (\"Hilton Grand Vacations\") is fine for factual reference. Do NOT use brand logos without permission.
- [ ] **Long-term process:** In production, all property details and photos come from the **owner** when they create a listing. Seed data is DEV-only (guarded by `IS_DEV_ENVIRONMENT`).

### 4. Terms of Service updates
- [ ] Add ToS clause: owners attest they have rights to uploaded content
- [ ] Add ToS clause: RAV is not affiliated with resort brands (already in disclaimers?)
- [ ] Consider DMCA takedown process for any reported infringement

### 5. Verify production safety
- [ ] Confirm seed data is DEV-only (DEC-019 says it should be)
- [ ] Confirm PROD database has no scraped content
- [ ] Verify `seed-manager` edge function is not deployed to PROD
- [ ] If PROD has seeded data: plan migration to remove/replace it

## Legal Framework
- **Facts are not copyrightable** (Feist Publications v. Rural Telephone, 1991) — resort names, locations, amenities are fine
- **Creative expression IS copyrightable** — marketing descriptions, photos, taglines
- **Trademark fair use** — mentioning brand names for identification purposes is generally permissible (nominative fair use)
- **Terms of use scraping restrictions** — some websites prohibit scraping in their ToS. While enforceability varies, best to not rely on scraped data in production.

## Priority
**HIGH** — This should be resolved before public launch. The risk is low if seed data stays DEV-only and production data comes from owners, but we need to verify this is the case.

## Dependencies
- #80 (Legal review — Terms of Service) — related but separate
- DEC-019 (Seed data is DEV-only) — confirms architectural intent

## Estimated Effort
~3-4 hours for audit + 2-4 hours for remediation if needed
title: Legal: Audit scraped resort data for compliance — review seed data sources

# Pitch Deck & Investor Presentation Materials

**Parent epic:** #250

## Objective
Create professional, VC-ready presentation materials that clearly communicate RAV's value proposition, market opportunity, competitive advantages, and traction. These materials serve multiple audiences: investors, potential partners, resort operators, and team recruitment.

## Deliverables

### 1. Pitch Deck (10-15 slides)
- [ ] **Cover:** Rent-A-Vacation — tagline, logo, brand colors
- [ ] **Problem:** Timeshare owners pay $1,200+/yr in maintenance fees for weeks they don't use. Travelers overpay through traditional booking channels.
- [ ] **Solution:** RAV is a two-sided marketplace connecting timeshare owners with travelers at up to 70% savings. \"Name Your Price\" bidding, AI-powered search, per-night pricing.
- [ ] **Market Size:** TAM/SAM/SOM (from #255 ARDA data). ~9.6M US timeshare owners, ~$10B industry, estimated $X in unused weeks annually.
- [ ] **How It Works:** Owner journey (list → receive offers → confirm → get paid) + Traveler journey (search → bid → book → check in). Visual flow diagram.
- [ ] **Product Demo:** Key screenshots: homepage, search/voice, PropertyDetail, bidding, owner dashboard, SmartEarn. Link to live demo or video.
- [ ] **Competitive Landscape:** Feature comparison matrix (from #251). Position RAV as the only platform with AI search + bidding + per-night pricing + full escrow.
- [ ] **Business Model:** 15% commission, tier-adjusted. Revenue per booking example. Path to profitability.
- [ ] **Traction:** Platform stats (825 tests, 30 edge functions, 46 migrations, 43 routes, 117 resorts, 10 destinations). Beta user feedback if available.
- [ ] **Technology:** Architecture overview (React + Supabase + Stripe + VAPI + Capacitor). AI-first platform. Built for scale.
- [ ] **Go-to-Market:** Owner acquisition strategy (Facebook Groups, ARDA network, direct outreach). Traveler acquisition (SEO, social, word-of-mouth, SmartEarn as funnel).
- [ ] **Team:** Founders, backgrounds, why this team wins.
- [ ] **Ask:** What you're raising, what it funds, milestones it unlocks.
- [ ] **Appendix:** Detailed financials, unit economics, feature roadmap (mobile app, SMS, API platform).

### 2. One-Pager (Leave-Behind)
- [ ] Single-page PDF summarizing problem, solution, market, traction, ask
- [ ] QR code linking to live platform
- [ ] Contact info

### 3. Demo Video (2-3 minutes)
- [ ] Screen recording with voiceover showing:
  - Traveler: browse → voice search → make offer → checkout
  - Owner: list property → receive offer → confirm → track earnings
  - SmartEarn calculator demonstration
- [ ] Professional feel (clean cuts, background music, captions)
- [ ] Tools: Loom, OBS Studio, or ScreenFlow for recording; CapCut or DaVinci Resolve for editing

### 4. Elevator Pitch Script (30 seconds + 2 minutes)
- [ ] 30-second version: \"Timeshare owners pay thousands annually for weeks they never use. Travelers overpay for the same resorts through hotels. Rent-A-Vacation is the marketplace that connects them — owners earn money, travelers save up to 70%. We're the only platform with AI voice search, Name Your Price bidding, and per-night pricing. 117 resorts across Hilton, Marriott, and Disney brands.\"
- [ ] 2-minute expanded version with competitive positioning and ask

## Brand Assets Available
- Logo, colors, typography: `docs/brand-assets/BRAND-STYLE-GUIDE.md`
- Marketing copy and audience profiles: `docs/brand-assets/MARKETING-PLAYBOOK.md`
- Existing pitch language: `docs/brand-assets/PITCH-DECK-SCRIPT.md`
- Boardroom advisory session: `docs/boardroom/` (strategic framing)
- Demo walkthrough script: `docs/DEMO-WALKTHROUGH.md`

## Tools for Creation
- **Slides:** Google Slides, Canva, or Pitch.com (all free/freemium)
- **Video:** Loom (free for 5 min), OBS Studio (free), CapCut (free editing)
- **One-pager:** Canva or Google Docs

## Dependencies
- #251 (Feature comparison matrix) — for competitive slide
- #255 (ARDA data) — for market size slide
- #253 (Review mining) — for \"problems we solve\" evidence

## Estimated Effort
~8-12 hours total (deck creation + video recording + iteration)

# Industry Reports & ARDA Data Collection

**Parent epic:** #250

## Objective
Gather authoritative industry data to support pitch deck claims, understand market size, and identify trends. VCs expect data-backed assertions about the market.

## Key Data Points Needed

### Market Size & Scope
- [ ] Total US timeshare market value (~$10B+ annually)
- [ ] Number of timeshare owners in the US (~9.6M per ARDA)
- [ ] Average annual maintenance fee ($1,120+ and rising)
- [ ] Percentage of weeks that go unused (estimated 50-85%)
- [ ] Total \"wasted\" maintenance fees annually (our TAM calculation)

### Industry Trends
- [ ] Year-over-year growth in timeshare ownership
- [ ] Rising maintenance fees trend (10-year data)
- [ ] Owner satisfaction scores (declining?)
- [ ] Resale market dynamics (timeshares lose 50-90% of value)
- [ ] Rental market growth rate

### Sources

#### ARDA (American Resort Development Association)
- **Website:** arda.org
- **Key publications:**
  - State of the Vacation Timeshare Industry (annual)
  - Resort Owners' Study
  - Economic Impact Study
- **Access options:**
  - Free: press releases, infographics, conference presentations (search \"ARDA timeshare statistics\")
  - Paid: Allied Membership (~$500/yr) — full reports + industry events + resort operator directory
  - Alternative: Some reports available for individual purchase ($200-500)
- **Action:** Check arda.org/research for currently available free content

#### Other Sources (Free)
- [ ] Perspective Group reports (timeshare consulting firm — publishes free insights)
- [ ] Ernst & Young timeshare industry studies (occasionally free)
- [ ] Wyndham, Marriott, Hilton annual reports (public companies — 10-K filings mention vacation ownership segment data)
- [ ] IBISWorld or Statista (may have free summaries)
- [ ] AIF Global (timeshare trade association for non-US markets)

#### Academic / Legal
- [ ] FTC reports on timeshare complaints
- [ ] State attorney general actions against timeshare companies (validates the problem)
- [ ] Consumer Financial Protection Bureau complaints database

## Deliverable
- \"Market Fact Sheet\" — one-pager with verified statistics, properly sourced
- TAM/SAM/SOM calculation for pitch deck
- Key quotes/stats for marketing materials
- Source bibliography (VCs may ask \"where did you get that number?\")

## Post-Incorporation Action
- Consider ARDA membership for: credibility, resort operator contacts, conference attendance, full report access
- ARDA Annual Conference (typically April/May) is prime networking for resort partnerships

## Estimated Effort
~3-4 hours of research + 1-2 hours to compile
title: Market Research: Industry reports & ARDA data collection

# Google Trends & Keyword Research

**Parent epic:** #250

## Objective
Understand search demand for timeshare rentals, identify seasonal patterns, and find the keywords that potential users are actually searching for. This informs SEO strategy, ad campaigns, and content marketing.

## Research Tasks

### Google Trends Analysis
- [ ] Compare search interest: \"timeshare rental\" vs \"rent timeshare\" vs \"timeshare for rent\"
- [ ] Compare: \"vacation rental\" vs \"timeshare rental\" vs \"resort rental\"
- [ ] Seasonal patterns: when do people search most? (likely Jan-Mar for summer travel)
- [ ] Geographic interest: which US states search most for timeshare rentals?
- [ ] Rising queries: what related searches are growing?
- [ ] Compare brand terms: \"Hilton timeshare rental\" vs \"Marriott timeshare rental\" vs \"Disney vacation club rental\"

### Keyword Research (Google Keyword Planner or free alternatives)
- [ ] Search volume for core terms:
  - \"timeshare rental\" / \"rent my timeshare\"
  - \"vacation ownership rental\"
  - \"resort rental by owner\"
  - \"cheap resort vacation\"
  - \"luxury resort deals\"
  - \"[brand] timeshare for rent\" (each of our 9 brands)
  - \"[destination] resort rental\" (each of our 10 destinations)
- [ ] Long-tail keywords with commercial intent:
  - \"how to rent out my timeshare\"
  - \"rent timeshare from owner\"
  - \"timeshare rental vs hotel\"
  - \"is renting a timeshare worth it\"
- [ ] Competitor brand keywords:

# Review Mining — Competitor Review Analysis

**Parent epic:** #250

## Objective
Analyze public reviews of competitor platforms to identify unmet user needs, common complaints, and feature requests — then validate that RAV addresses them.

## Sources to Mine

### App Store Reviews (if mobile apps exist)
- [ ] Koala — iOS App Store + Google Play
- [ ] RedWeek — iOS App Store + Google Play
- [ ] Timeshares Only — check for apps
- [ ] VRBO / Airbnb — focus on timeshare-specific complaints

### Web Reviews
- [ ] Trustpilot reviews for each competitor
- [ ] BBB (Better Business Bureau) complaints and ratings
- [ ] Google Business Profile reviews
- [ ] Reddit: r/timeshare, r/VacationRentals, r/travel (search for competitor mentions)
- [ ] Facebook Group discussions about competitor experiences

### What to Look For
1. **Payment complaints** — hidden fees, payment delays, refund issues
2. **Trust issues** — scams, unresponsive owners, fake listings
3. **UX complaints** — hard to navigate, outdated design, poor mobile experience
4. **Feature requests** — \"I wish this platform had...\"
5. **Positive signals** — what do happy users praise? (so we can match or exceed it)

## Analysis Framework

For each competitor, categorize findings:

| Category | Complaint/Request | Frequency | RAV Status |
|----------|------------------|-----------|------------|
| Payments | \"Took 3 weeks to get my refund\" | High | ✅ Stripe escrow with automated refunds |
| Trust | \"No way to verify the owner is real\" | Medium | ✅ Identity verification built |
| Pricing | \"Hidden fees at checkout\" | High | ✅ Full fee breakdown on PropertyDetail + Checkout |
| Search | \"Can't find what I'm looking for\" | Medium | ✅ AI voice + text search |
| Mobile | \"No mobile app\" | High | 🔜 Capacitor app planned (#240) |

## Deliverable
- Spreadsheet with categorized review findings (aim for 50-100 data points across all competitors)
- \"RAV solves this\" mapping for pitch deck
- Unaddressed pain points → new feature ideas for backlog

## Team Activity
This works great as a group session — everyone picks a source, spends 30 min mining reviews, then reconvene to discuss patterns.

## Estimated Effort
~3-4 hours across the team
title: Market Research: Review mining — extract insights from competitor reviews

## Objective
Conduct thorough market research to understand the competitive landscape, validate RAV's positioning, and build materials for VC conversations, partner discussions, and go-to-market strategy.

- VCs and potential partners are asking questions about market positioning
- Need to articulate differentiation clearly in pitch materials
- Post-launch strategy should be informed by what competitors do well and poorly
- Team alignment on competitive positioning accelerates decision-making

## RAV's Key Differentiators (Identified)
These set RAV apart from every known competitor:
1. **"Name Your Price" bidding engine** — no competitor offers this
2. **AI voice + text search (RAVIO)** — first in the timeshare rental space
3. **Dynamic pricing intelligence** — SmartEarn, SmartCompare, SmartPrice, SmartMatch, SmartBudget
4. **Per-night pricing** — competitors price by the week; RAV offers flexibility
5. **Full escrow + Stripe Connect payouts** — most competitors handle payments off-platform
6. **Proactive notifications + SMS reminders** — engagement, not passive listing
7. **15% commission** — competitive with or lower than most platforms
8. **Branded mobile app** (planned) — most competitors are web-only or have dated apps

## Sub-Issues

### Research Activities
- [ ] #251 — Feature comparison matrix (RAV vs. competitors)
- [ ] #252 — Mystery shopping: create accounts on 5+ competitor platforms
- [ ] #253 — Review mining: extract insights from competitor app/web reviews

- [ ] #257 — Scraped resort data audit — legal compliance review

|-----------|------|-------|
| **Koala.com** | Timeshare rental marketplace | Most direct competitor |
| **RedWeek.com** | Timeshare rental + resale | Established, large inventory |
| **Timeshares Only** | Rental + resale | Traditional listing board |
| **SellMyTimeshare.com** | Resale + rental side | Older platform |

### Apple App Store
- [ ] Enroll in Apple Developer Program ($99/year) — requires LLC for Organization account

  - Description: Value prop, features, how it works (use MARKETING-PLAYBOOK.md)

  - Support URL: `https://rent-a-vacation.com/contact`

- [ ] Screenshots (required sizes):
  - iPhone 6.7\" (1290x2796) — at least 3, up to 10
  - iPhone 6.5\" (1242x2688) — at least 3
  - iPad 12.9\" (2048x2732) — if supporting iPad
  - Key screens: Home, Search Results, Property Detail, Booking, Owner Dashboard

### Apple Review Considerations (IMPORTANT)

- [ ] Provide demo account credentials for App Review team

- [ ] Data safety section:
  - Data collected and shared declarations
  - Encryption in transit: Yes
  - Data deletion available: Yes (GDPR delete account feature already built)
- [ ] Generate signed AAB (Android App Bundle) and upload
- [ ] Target API level 34+ (Google's current requirement)

### OTA Updates (Capacitor Live Update)
- [ ] Evaluate Capgo or Ionic Appflow for over-the-air JS bundle updates
- [ ] Benefit: most updates don't require store resubmission (faster iteration)
- [ ] Only native plugin changes require full store update

- [ ] Android: Fastlane or manual AAB upload to Play Console

# Offline Support & Native Storage

**Parent epic:** #240

## User Stories

> As a traveler browsing listings at a resort with spotty WiFi, I want to still see listings I've already viewed so I can compare options without losing my progress.

> As a property owner filling out a listing form on my phone, I want my draft to be saved automatically so I don't lose my work if the app crashes or I lose connection.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Offline listing cache
- [ ] Cache recently viewed listings in `@capacitor/preferences` or IndexedDB
- [ ] Show cached listings when offline with a \"You're offline — showing cached data\" banner
- [ ] Cache listing images for offline viewing (limited to last 20 viewed)
- [ ] Favorites/saved listings always available offline
- [ ] Search results page shows \"Connect to search for new listings\" when offline

### Form draft persistence
- [ ] Auto-save ListProperty form state to local storage every 30 seconds
- [ ] Restore draft on app reopen with \"Resume your draft?\" prompt
- [ ] Auto-save BidFormDialog draft (amount, dates, message)
- [ ] Clear drafts after successful submission

### Offline submission queue
- [ ] Install `@capacitor/network` plugin for connection monitoring
- [ ] Create `useOfflineQueue()` hook:
  - Queue failed API submissions (bids, messages, profile updates)
  - Show \"Queued — will submit when online\" toast
  - Auto-retry when connection returns
  - Show pending queue count in UI
- [ ] Handle conflicts: if a listing is no longer available when queue syncs, notify user

### Connection status
- [ ] Create `useNetworkStatus()` hook wrapping `@capacitor/network`
- [ ] Show subtle banner when offline (not intrusive — users may be on airplane mode intentionally)
- [ ] Disable actions that require connectivity (checkout, new listing submission) with clear messaging
- [ ] React Query: configure `networkMode: 'offlineFirst'` for read queries

### Native storage migration
- [ ] Migrate from `localStorage` to `@capacitor/preferences` for persistent data on native
- [ ] Keep `localStorage` as fallback for web
- [ ] Encrypt sensitive stored data (auth tokens, user preferences)

## Technical Notes
- React Query already has excellent offline support — configure `gcTime` and `staleTime` appropriately
- `@capacitor/preferences` is async (unlike localStorage) — wrap in utility functions
- Image caching: use Capacitor Filesystem to store blobs, reference by listing ID
- Offline queue: consider a simple IndexedDB-backed queue with retry logic

## Estimated Effort
~8-10 hours

## Dependencies
- #241 (Capacitor shell)
- #243 (Native auth — for secure storage patterns)

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Recently viewed listings display when offline
- [ ] Form drafts survive app kills
- [ ] Failed submissions queue and retry automatically
- [ ] Clear offline indicator when disconnected
- [ ] No data loss on connection drops mid-action

# Deep Linking & Universal Links

**Parent epic:** #240

## User Stories

> As a traveler, I want to tap a shared listing link on my phone and have it open directly in the RAV app (if installed) so I get the best experience.

> As a user who received an email notification, I want to tap the link and land on the right screen in the app, not the mobile website.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Universal Links (iOS) / App Links (Android)
- [ ] Host `/.well-known/apple-app-site-association` on `rent-a-vacation.com` (via Vercel)
- [ ] Host `/.well-known/assetlinks.json` on `rent-a-vacation.com` (via Vercel)
- [ ] Configure Associated Domains in Xcode (`applinks:rent-a-vacation.com`)
- [ ] Configure `intent-filter` in Android `AndroidManifest.xml` for `rent-a-vacation.com`
- [ ] Verify: tapping `https://rent-a-vacation.com/property/123` opens the app

### Deep link routing
- [ ] Install `@capacitor/app` plugin for app URL handling
- [ ] Create `useDeepLink()` hook that listens for incoming URLs and routes to React Router
- [ ] Map all public routes to deep links:
  | URL Pattern | App Screen |
  |---|---|
  | `/property/:id` | PropertyDetail |
  | `/rentals` | Rentals (search) |
  | `/destinations/:slug` | DestinationDetail |
  | `/destinations/:slug/:city` | DestinationDetail (city) |
  | `/checkout` | Checkout |
  | `/my-trips` | RenterDashboard |
  | `/owner-dashboard` | OwnerDashboard |
  | `/notifications` | Notifications |
  | `/calculator` | SmartEarn Calculator |
  | `/tools/*` | RAV Tools |
- [ ] Handle authenticated routes: if deep link targets a protected route and user is not logged in, show login then redirect

### Share functionality
- [ ] Install `@capacitor/share` plugin
- [ ] Add \"Share\" button on PropertyDetail page (mobile only or enhanced on mobile)
- [ ] Share payload: title + description + URL (e.g., \"Check out this Hilton resort at 60% off!\")
- [ ] Add share button on DestinationDetail page
- [ ] Track shares in analytics (PostHog custom event)

### Email integration
- [ ] Update email templates: links should use `https://rent-a-vacation.com/...` URLs (these will auto-open in app via universal links)
- [ ] Test: email links open app on iOS and Android when installed
- [ ] Fallback: if app not installed, links open in mobile browser (existing behavior)

## Technical Notes
- Vercel supports `.well-known` files via `vercel.json` rewrites or `public/.well-known/` directory
- Universal Links require HTTPS (already in place)
- Deep links received while app is cold-started need special handling (`App.addListener('appUrlOpen', ...)`)
- Consider deferred deep links for install attribution (user clicks link → installs app → opens to that content)

## Estimated Effort
~4-6 hours

## Dependencies
- #241 (Capacitor shell)
- Domain access for `.well-known` files (Vercel config)

## Definition of Done
- [ ] `rent-a-vacation.com` links open in the app when installed (iOS + Android)
- [ ] All public routes are deep-linkable
- [ ] Share button works on PropertyDetail with native share sheet
- [ ] Email notification links open the app
- [ ] Graceful fallback to browser if app not installed

# Camera Integration & Image Capture

**Parent epic:** #240

## User Stories

> As a property owner, I want to take photos of my resort unit directly from the app so I can list my property without needing to transfer photos from my camera roll.

> As a user completing identity verification, I want to capture my ID document using the camera so I can verify my identity without scanning or emailing documents.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Camera plugin setup
- [ ] Install `@capacitor/camera` plugin
- [ ] Configure iOS `NSCameraUsageDescription`: \"RAV needs camera access to photograph your property\"
- [ ] Configure iOS `NSPhotoLibraryUsageDescription`: \"RAV needs photo library access to upload property images\"
- [ ] Configure Android camera permissions in `AndroidManifest.xml`

### Property photo capture
- [ ] Add \"Take Photo\" button alongside existing \"Upload\" in PropertyImageUpload component
- [ ] On Capacitor: show action sheet with options: \"Take Photo\", \"Choose from Library\", \"Cancel\"
- [ ] On web: fall back to existing file input (no change)
- [ ] Camera captures at optimal resolution (max 2048px width — balances quality vs upload size)
- [ ] Auto-compress images before upload (target: < 500KB per image, JPEG quality 80%)
- [ ] Show upload progress indicator (especially important on mobile data connections)
- [ ] Support capturing multiple photos in sequence without returning to the form each time

### Image handling
- [ ] Create `useImageCapture()` hook:
  ```typescript
  const { captureImage, pickFromGallery, isCapturing } = useImageCapture({
    maxWidth: 2048,
    quality: 80,
    allowMultiple: true,
  });
  ```
- [ ] Handle EXIF orientation (some phones save images rotated — normalize before upload)
- [ ] Strip EXIF location data for privacy before uploading to Supabase Storage
- [ ] Retry failed uploads with exponential backoff (mobile connections can be flaky)
- [ ] Queue uploads if offline, sync when connection returns

### Verification document capture
- [ ] Extend EvidenceUpload component with camera option on mobile
- [ ] Add document capture mode: guide overlay showing ID card placement area
- [ ] Ensure captured documents are readable (minimum resolution check)

### Gallery improvements
- [ ] Pinch-to-zoom on property images in PropertyDetail
- [ ] Full-screen image viewer with swipe between images
- [ ] Reorder images via drag-and-drop in edit mode

## Technical Notes
- `@capacitor/camera` returns base64 or file URI — convert to Blob for Supabase Storage upload
- On web, the hook falls back to `<input type=\"file\" accept=\"image/*\" capture>` which lets mobile browsers offer camera
- Image compression can use canvas API (no external library needed)
- Consider `@capacitor/filesystem` for temporary storage during multi-photo capture sessions

## Estimated Effort
~6-8 hours

## Dependencies
- #241 (Capacitor shell)

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Owner can take photos and upload directly from the app
- [ ] Images are compressed to < 500KB before upload
- [ ] EXIF data (especially GPS) stripped before upload
- [ ] Upload progress visible to user
- [ ] Works on both iOS and Android
- [ ] Falls back gracefully to file picker on web

# Push Notifications — FCM & APNs Integration

**Parent epic:** #240

## User Stories

> As a property owner, I want to receive a push notification on my phone when someone makes an offer on my listing so I can respond quickly and not lose the booking.

> As a traveler, I want to get a push notification when my offer is accepted so I can complete payment before the hold expires.

> As a user, I want to control which push notifications I receive so I'm not overwhelmed.

## Why This Matters
Push notifications are the **#1 reason** to have a native app over a PWA. Time-sensitive marketplace events (offers, acceptances, payment deadlines, check-in reminders) need immediate user attention. Email has 20-30% open rates; push notifications have 60-90%.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Infrastructure setup
- [ ] Install `@capacitor/push-notifications` plugin
- [ ] Create Firebase project for FCM (Android + iOS through APNs proxy)
- [ ] Configure APNs key in Firebase (for iOS push via FCM)
- [ ] Add `google-services.json` (Android) and `GoogleService-Info.plist` (iOS) to native projects
- [ ] Create `push-register` edge function: receives device token + user ID, stores in `user_devices` table
- [ ] Migration: Create `user_devices` table (`id`, `user_id`, `platform`, `device_token`, `created_at`, `last_active_at`)

### Client-side implementation
- [ ] Create `usePushNotifications()` hook:
  - Request permission on first app open (iOS shows system dialog)
  - Register device token with backend on success
  - Re-register on token refresh (tokens can change)
  - Handle notification received while app is in foreground (show in-app toast, don't duplicate with NotificationBell)
  - Handle notification tap (deep link to relevant screen)
  - Unregister on sign out
- [ ] Map notification types to deep links:
  | Notification | Deep Link |
  |---|---|
  | `new_bid_received` | `/owner-dashboard?tab=bookings-earnings` |
  | `bid_accepted` | `/checkout` |
  | `bid_rejected` | `/my-trips?tab=offers` |
  | `booking_confirmed` | `/my-trips?tab=bookings` |
  | `payment_received` | `/owner-dashboard?tab=bookings-earnings` |
  | `message_received` | `/my-trips?tab=bookings` (with thread open) |
  | `check_in_reminder` | `/checkin` |
  | `idle_listing_alert` | `/owner-dashboard?tab=my-listings` |

### Backend: Send push notifications
- [ ] Extend `notification-dispatcher` edge function:
  - After in-app + email delivery, check if user has registered devices
  - Send push via FCM HTTP v1 API for each device
  - Include: title, body, data payload (notification type + entity IDs for deep linking)
  - Handle invalid/expired tokens (remove from `user_devices`)
  - Respect user notification preferences (existing `user_notification_preferences` table — add `push` channel)
- [ ] Add `push` to notification channel options in `notification_catalog` table

### Preference sync
- [ ] Update NotificationPreferences page (`/settings/notifications`):
  - Add "Push Notifications" column alongside Email and SMS
  - Per-notification-type toggle for push channel
  - Master push toggle (enable/disable all push)
- [ ] Sync preferences with existing `user_notification_preferences` table

### iOS-specific
- [ ] Handle provisional notifications (iOS 12+): can deliver quietly to Notification Center without explicit permission
- [ ] Configure notification categories for actionable notifications:
  - `NEW_OFFER`: actions = [Accept, View Details]
  - `CHECK_IN`: actions = [Start Check-in]
- [ ] Badge count: update app icon badge with unread notification count
- [ ] Clear badge on app open

### Android-specific
- [ ] Create notification channels:
  - `offers` (high importance): new offers, acceptances, rejections
  - `bookings` (high importance): confirmations, payment, check-in
  - `messages` (default importance): messages from owners/travelers
  - `marketing` (low importance): tips, suggestions, seasonal
- [ ] Custom notification sound (optional, use brand chime if available)
- [ ] Large icon: use RAV logo

## Technical Notes

### FCM HTTP v1 API (in notification-dispatcher)
```typescript
// Example push payload
const message = {
  token: deviceToken,
  notification: {
    title: 'New Offer Received!',
    body: 'A traveler offered $1,200 for your Hilton Hawaiian Village listing',
  },
  data: {
    type: 'new_bid_received',
    bid_id: '...',
    listing_id: '...',
  },
  android: {
    notification: { channel_id: 'offers' }
  },
  apns: {
    payload: { aps: { badge: unreadCount, sound: 'default' } }
  }
};
```

### Permission request timing
Don't ask for push permission immediately on install. Wait until the user completes their first meaningful action (e.g., creates a listing, makes an offer, completes a booking). This increases permission grant rates from ~50% to ~80%.

## Estimated Effort
~10-14 hours (largest mobile story)

## Dependencies
- #241 (Capacitor shell)
- #240 (Epic)
- Firebase project creation (manual, ~30 min)
- Apple Developer account for APNs key

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Push notifications arrive on iOS and Android for all notification types
- [ ] Tapping a notification opens the correct screen in the app
- [ ] Users can control push preferences per notification type
- [ ] Invalid tokens are cleaned up automatically
- [ ] Push only sends if user has opted in for that notification type

# Native Authentication — Biometric Login & OAuth Deeplinks

**Parent epic:** #240

## User Stories

> As a returning user, I want to unlock the app with Face ID or my fingerprint so I don't have to type my password every time.

> As a user signing up with Google, I want the OAuth flow to return me to the app (not the browser) so the experience feels seamless.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Biometric authentication
- [ ] Install `@capacitor-community/biometric-auth` or `@capacitor/biometrics` plugin
- [ ] On successful email/password or OAuth login, prompt: \"Enable Face ID / Fingerprint for faster login?\"
- [ ] Store auth refresh token in secure native storage (`@capacitor/preferences` with encryption or iOS Keychain / Android Keystore)
- [ ] On app launch (if biometric enabled): show biometric prompt → retrieve stored token → resume session
- [ ] Fallback to email/password if biometric fails 3 times or is unavailable
- [ ] Settings toggle to enable/disable biometric auth (in Account Settings)
- [ ] Handle device biometric enrollment changes (e.g., new fingerprint added — re-authenticate)

### OAuth deeplinks
- [ ] Configure Universal Links (iOS) and App Links (Android) for `rent-a-vacation.com`
- [ ] Host `.well-known/apple-app-site-association` and `.well-known/assetlinks.json` on the web domain
- [ ] Update Supabase Auth redirect URL to use app scheme: `com.rentavacation.app://auth/callback`
- [ ] Register custom URL scheme `rentavacation://` as fallback
- [ ] Update `signInWithGoogle()` in AuthContext to detect Capacitor and use `@capacitor/browser` for OAuth flow
- [ ] Handle `PASSWORD_RECOVERY` deep link so password reset emails open the app directly
- [ ] Test: Google OAuth → browser → callback → app resumes with session

### Session management
- [ ] Persist Supabase session in secure native storage (not localStorage which can be cleared)
- [ ] Auto-refresh tokens in background using Supabase's `onAuthStateChange`
- [ ] Handle app returning from background: verify session is still valid
- [ ] If session expired while in background: show biometric prompt or login screen (not a blank screen)

### Security
- [ ] Never store raw passwords — only refresh tokens
- [ ] Encrypt stored credentials using platform keychain (iOS Keychain / Android Keystore)
- [ ] Clear stored credentials on sign out
- [ ] Rate limit biometric attempts (3 failures → require password)
- [ ] Log auth events to Sentry for monitoring

## Technical Notes

### AuthContext changes needed
```typescript
// In AuthContext.tsx, add platform-aware session persistence:
import { Capacitor } from '@capacitor/core';
import { Preferences } from '@capacitor/preferences';

// On login success:
if (Capacitor.isNativePlatform()) {
  await Preferences.set({ key: 'supabase_refresh_token', value: session.refresh_token });
}

// On app launch:
if (Capacitor.isNativePlatform()) {
  const { value } = await Preferences.get({ key: 'supabase_refresh_token' });
  if (value) {
    // Attempt biometric → restore session
  }
}
```

### Supabase Auth config
- Add `com.rentavacation.app://auth/callback` to Supabase Auth redirect URLs in dashboard
- Add `rentavacation://auth/callback` as additional redirect URL

## Estimated Effort
~6-8 hours

## Dependencies
- #241 (Capacitor shell setup)

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Returning users can unlock with Face ID / fingerprint (iOS + Android)
- [ ] Google OAuth flow works end-to-end without leaving the app context
- [ ] Password reset emails open the app directly (not browser)
- [ ] Session persists across app kills and device restarts
- [ ] Biometric can be toggled on/off in Account Settings

# Mobile UX Polish

**Parent epic:** #240

## User Story
> As a mobile user, I want the app to feel native with proper safe area handling, gesture navigation, and a thumb-friendly bottom tab bar so the experience matches what I expect from iOS and Android apps.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Safe areas & notch handling
- [ ] Install `@capacitor/status-bar` plugin
- [ ] Apply `env(safe-area-inset-*)` CSS for content padding on notched devices
- [ ] Header doesn't overlap with iOS Dynamic Island or Android camera cutout
- [ ] Bottom navigation doesn't overlap with iOS home indicator or Android gesture bar
- [ ] Landscape mode renders correctly (or lock to portrait if not needed)

### Bottom tab navigation (mobile only)
- [ ] Create `MobileTabBar` component (visible only on Capacitor, hidden on web)
- [ ] Tab items based on user role:
  - **Traveler:** Home, Search, My Trips, Notifications, Account
  - **Owner:** Home, Listings, Bookings, Notifications, Account
  - **Admin:** (uses existing full nav — no tab bar)
- [ ] Active tab indicator with brand teal color
- [ ] Badge on Notifications tab showing unread count
- [ ] Tab bar uses haptic feedback on tap (via `@capacitor/haptics`)
- [ ] Smooth transitions between tabs (no full page reload)

### Touch optimization
- [ ] All interactive elements meet 44x44pt minimum tap target (WCAG)
- [ ] Add pull-to-refresh on listing pages, dashboard, notifications (`@capacitor/app` or custom)
- [ ] Swipe-to-go-back works on iOS (default WebView behavior — verify it doesn't conflict)
- [ ] Long-press on listing card shows quick actions (share, favorite, compare)
- [ ] Image galleries support pinch-to-zoom and swipe

### Typography & spacing
- [ ] Increase base font size on mobile to 16px minimum (prevents iOS auto-zoom on input focus)
- [ ] Ensure form inputs have `font-size: 16px` to prevent iOS zoom
- [ ] Adjust card padding for thumb-friendly spacing
- [ ] Dialog/sheet components slide up from bottom (not center modal) on mobile

### Performance
- [ ] Implement list virtualization for long listing grids (react-window or similar)
- [ ] Lazy load images below the fold
- [ ] Skeleton loading states for all data-fetching views
- [ ] Measure and optimize Time to Interactive on mid-range Android devices

## Technical Notes
- The existing `useIsMobile()` hook (768px breakpoint) can be extended to detect Capacitor
- shadcn/ui `Sheet` component already slides from bottom — reuse for mobile dialogs
- Consider `@capacitor/keyboard` plugin to handle keyboard showing/hiding and avoid layout shifts

## Estimated Effort
~8-12 hours across multiple passes

## Definition of Done
- [ ] App feels native on both iOS and Android — no visible WebView artifacts
- [ ] Bottom tab bar navigates between main sections smoothly
- [ ] All tap targets pass accessibility audit (44pt minimum)
- [ ] Pull-to-refresh works on key pages
- [ ] No iOS auto-zoom on form inputs

# Capacitor Shell & Project Setup

**Parent epic:** #240

## User Story
> As a developer, I want to initialize Capacitor in the existing React project so that the web app can run as a native iOS and Android app with access to device APIs.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Project initialization
- [ ] Install Capacitor core: `@capacitor/core`, `@capacitor/cli`
- [ ] Run `npx cap init` with app ID `com.rentavacation.app` and name `Rent-A-Vacation`
- [ ] Configure `capacitor.config.ts` with webDir pointing to Vite's `dist/` output
- [ ] Add iOS platform: `npx cap add ios`
- [ ] Add Android platform: `npx cap add android`
- [ ] Verify `npx cap sync` runs without errors after `npm run build`

### App icons & splash screens
- [ ] Generate all required icon sizes from existing `android-chrome-512x512.png`:
  - iOS: 20pt through 1024pt (all @1x, @2x, @3x variants)
  - Android: mdpi through xxxhdpi (48-192px)
- [ ] Create splash screen with RAV logo + brand colors (teal `#0d6b5c` background)
- [ ] Use `@capacitor/splash-screen` plugin for native splash control
- [ ] Configure splash auto-hide after app loads (not on WebView ready)

### Native configuration
- [ ] iOS: Set deployment target to iOS 15+ (covers 95%+ of active devices)
- [ ] Android: Set minSdkVersion 24 (Android 7.0+, covers 95%+)
- [ ] Configure status bar style (light content on teal background)
- [ ] Set `allowsLinkPreview: false` to prevent iOS long-press previews of internal links
- [ ] Set `overrideUserAgent` to include `RAV-Mobile/1.0` for analytics differentiation
- [ ] Configure keyboard behavior: `resize: 'ionic'` or `resize: 'native'`

### Build pipeline
- [ ] Add npm scripts: `cap:build`, `cap:sync`, `cap:open:ios`, `cap:open:android`
- [ ] Document build workflow: `npm run build` → `npx cap sync` → open in Xcode/Android Studio
- [ ] Verify hot reload works via `npx cap run` for development
- [ ] Test on physical device (at least one iOS + one Android)

### Platform detection
- [ ] Install `@capacitor/device` plugin
- [ ] Create `usePlatform()` hook returning `'web' | 'ios' | 'android'`
- [ ] Use platform detection to conditionally enable/disable native features
- [ ] Hide PWA install banner when running inside Capacitor

## Technical Notes
- Vite's `base: '/'` config is compatible with Capacitor
- Existing service worker (vite-plugin-pwa) should be disabled in Capacitor mode (native handles caching)
- React Router's `BrowserRouter` works in Capacitor — no changes needed
- Supabase JS SDK works in Capacitor WebView — no changes needed

## Estimated Effort
~4-6 hours for setup + icon generation + initial device testing

## Definition of Done
- [ ] `npx cap run ios` launches the app on iOS simulator with RAV splash screen
- [ ] `npx cap run android` launches the app on Android emulator with RAV splash screen
- [ ] All existing routes and features work identically in the native shell
- [ ] `usePlatform()` hook correctly identifies native vs web
- [ ] Build pipeline documented

body:
# Mobile App Epic — iOS & Android via Capacitor

## Strategic Context

VCs and early partners consistently ask: "Do you have a mobile app?" A native mobile presence signals product maturity, unlocks push notifications (the most effective engagement channel), and provides App Store / Play Store discoverability.

**Decision:** DEC-011 — Native mobile via Capacitor (not React Native). Capacitor wraps our existing React SPA in a native shell, reusing 95%+ of the codebase while adding native device APIs.

## Current Mobile Readiness (Assessment April 1, 2026)

| Area | Status | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| Responsive UI | ✅ Good | Tailwind breakpoints, mobile nav, grid layouts |
| PWA | ✅ Ready | Manifest, service worker, install banner |
| Auth | ⚠️ Partial | Email/OAuth work, no biometric, no deeplinks |
| Push Notifications | ❌ Missing | In-app only (websocket), no native push |
| Camera | ❌ Missing | File upload only, no direct capture |
| Offline | ⚠️ Basic | PWA caching, no submission queue |
| Voice Search | ✅ Ready | VAPI works in WebView |
| App Store | ❌ Not started | No accounts, no assets |

## Architecture: What Capacitor Gives Us

```
┌──────────────────────────────────┐
│         Native Shell             │
│  (iOS: WKWebView / Android: WebView) │
├──────────────────────────────────┤
│      Capacitor Bridge            │
│  ┌─────────┬─────────┬────────┐  │
│  │ Camera  │  Push   │ Bio-   │  │
│  │ Plugin  │ Notif   │ metric │  │
│  ├─────────┼─────────┼────────┤  │
│  │ Deep    │ Secure  │ Status │  │
│  │ Links   │ Storage │  Bar   │  │
│  └─────────┴─────────┴────────┘  │
├──────────────────────────────────┤
│      Existing React SPA         │
│   (same codebase, same build)    │
└──────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Sub-Issues (8 stories)

### Phase A: Foundation (must-have for demo)
- [ ] #241 — Capacitor Shell & Project Setup (~4-6 hrs)
- [ ] #242 — Mobile UX Polish — safe areas, gestures, bottom navigation (~8-12 hrs)
- [ ] #243 — Native Authentication — biometric login & OAuth deeplinks (~6-8 hrs)

### Phase B: Core Native Features (must-have for store submission)
- [ ] #244 — Push Notifications — FCM & APNs integration (~10-14 hrs)
- [ ] #245 — Camera Integration & Image Capture (~6-8 hrs)
- [ ] #246 — Deep Linking & Universal Links (~4-6 hrs)

### Phase C: Enhanced Experience (post-initial release)
- [ ] #247 — Offline Support & Native Storage (~8-10 hrs)
- [ ] #248 — App Store Submission — iOS & Google Play (~6-10 hrs)

**Total estimated effort: ~53-74 hours across all stories**

## Cost & Accounts Required

| Item | Cost | Frequency |
|------|------|-----------|
| Apple Developer Program | $99 | Annual |
| Google Play Developer | $25 | One-time |
| Apple hardware (for iOS builds) | ~$0 if using CI | — |
| CI/CD (Appflow or GitHub Actions) | $0-49/mo | Monthly |

## Dependencies
- #127 (LLC/EIN) — Apple requires a legal entity for organization accounts
- Privacy Policy (#80) — both stores require it
- Social media profiles (#230-234) — store listings link to social

## VC Demo Strategy
Capacitor allows a **working demo on a physical phone** before store submission. Phase A alone (~18-26 hrs) produces a demo-ready app that runs natively with biometric login. Show investors the real app running on your phone — not a mockup.

body:
## Objective
Create a Google Business Profile for Rent-A-Vacation to improve brand search visibility, appear in Google's knowledge panel, and build trust with searchers.

## Why Google Business Profile (often overlooked, high impact)
- When someone Googles "Rent A Vacation", a Google Business Profile shows a knowledge panel on the right side of search results — logo, description, website link, reviews
- Establishes legitimacy immediately (users trust businesses with Google profiles)
- Free and takes 30 minutes to set up
- Improves local SEO signals even for online-only businesses
- Enables Google Reviews (social proof in search results)

## Context
Sentry is currently frontend-only (`@sentry/react`). Our 30 Supabase edge functions (Deno runtime) have no error tracking — failures are only visible in Supabase function logs, which are ephemeral and lack alerting.

## What to build
1. **Sentry Deno SDK** integration for edge functions (or lightweight HTTP transport)
2. **Shared helper** in `supabase/functions/_shared/sentry.ts` — init + captureException wrapper
3. **Instrument critical edge functions first:**
   - `stripe-webhook` (payment failures)
   - `process-cancellation` (refund failures)
   - `notification-dispatcher` (delivery failures)
   - `api-gateway` (public API errors)
4. **Environment tagging** — DEV vs PROD based on project ref

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Sentry captures unhandled exceptions from edge functions
- [ ] Errors include function name, request context, and environment
- [ ] Source maps uploaded for edge function code (if feasible with Deno)
- [ ] At minimum, the 4 critical functions listed above are instrumented
- [ ] Document setup in `docs/DEPLOYMENT.md`

## Technical notes
- Sentry has a Deno SDK (`@sentry/deno`) — verify compatibility with Supabase edge runtime
- Alternative: lightweight HTTP POST to Sentry ingest API if SDK doesn't work
- Consider a separate Sentry project for backend vs frontend, or use the same project with `server` tag
- Free tier budget: shared 10K transactions/month with frontend (may need to adjust sample rates)

## References
- Frontend config: `src/lib/sentry.ts`
- Edge function shared code: `supabase/functions/_shared/`
- Sentry org: `rent-a-vacation-org`
title: Sentry: Instrument Supabase edge functions for server-side error tracking

body:
## Summary

Introduce a **volume-based commission discount** for property owners that rewards repeat successful bookings. This is separate from (and stacks with) membership tier discounts.

## Design Decisions

### Trigger Metric
- Based on **completed bookings** (payouts received), not active listings
- Only counts bookings where the owner received payout — ensures real transactions

### Proposed Tiers (configurable by admin)
Individual timeshare owners typically have 1-3 properties, so thresholds should be achievable:

| Completed Bookings (annual) | Commission Discount |
|-----|-----|
| 10+ | -0.25% |
| 20+ | -0.50% |
| 40+ | -1.00% |

Base rate: 15% (from `system_settings.default_commission_rate`)

**Example:** An owner with 25 completed bookings on a Plus membership:
- 15% (base) - 2% (Plus tier) - 0.25% (volume) = **12.75%** effective commission

### Key Design Questions to Resolve

1. **Reset period:** Annual (calendar year) vs. rolling 12 months vs. permanent/lifetime?
   - Annual aligns with tax reporting but penalizes owners who join mid-year
   - Rolling 12-month is fairer but more complex to compute
   - Lifetime is simplest but reduces long-term revenue
   
2. **Tier thresholds:** The values above (10/20/40) are starting points. Should be admin-configurable in `system_settings` or a new `volume_discount_tiers` table.

3. **Stacking with membership:** Confirmed — volume discounts stack with membership tier discounts. Non-members still eligible for volume discounts.

4. **Visibility:** Should owners see their progress toward the next tier? (e.g., "7 more bookings to unlock -0.25% commission")

### Implementation Notes

- Needs new DB table or `system_settings` entries for configurable tier thresholds
- Count query: `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM bookings WHERE owner_id = ? AND status = 'completed' AND payout_status = 'paid' AND created_at >= [period_start]`
- Apply discount in `computeFeeBreakdown()` in `src/lib/pricing.ts` (already handles membership tier discounts)
- Display in Checkout fee breakdown, PropertyDetail sidebar, and OwnerDashboard earnings
- Admin UI: new section in system settings for managing volume tiers

### Related
- Stacks with membership tier discounts (Pro -2%, Business -5%)
- See also #155 (membership value proposition review)
- FAQ already says "with tiered discounts" — forward-compatible

comments.body:
**Reviewed: April 17, 2026 — Sujit**

This feature needs a discussion between **Sujit and Ajumon** before implementation. The following design decisions are open:

### Topics to Discuss

1. **Reset period:** Annual (calendar year) vs. rolling 12 months vs. lifetime accumulation
   - Annual penalizes owners joining mid-year
   - Rolling is fairer but more complex
   - Lifetime reduces long-term revenue

2. **Tier thresholds:** Are 10/20/40 completed bookings realistic for individual timeshare owners who typically have 1-3 properties? May need lower thresholds.

3. **Stacking behavior:** Volume discounts stack with membership tier discounts (Pro -2%, Business -5%). Is that the right approach, or should there be a floor on commission rate?

4. **Progress visibility:** Should owners see a progress bar toward the next discount tier? (e.g., "7 more bookings to unlock -0.25%")

5. **Business impact:** With the new tier features (#278-282 now shipped — early access, exclusive deals, priority placement, concierge, account manager), does volume discount still make sense as a separate incentive, or should it fold into the tier system?

### Implementation is straightforward once decisions are made
- New `volume_discount_tiers` config in system_settings (admin-configurable)
- Count query on completed+paid bookings within the chosen period
- Apply in `computeFeeBreakdown()` in `src/lib/pricing.ts`
- Display in Checkout, PropertyDetail, and OwnerDashboard

**Status:** Blocked on business decision. Assign to next Sujit + Ajumon sync.

title: Owner Volume Discount: tiered commission reduction based on completed bookings

Stripe Tax cannot be fully activated until a business entity is formed and state tax registrations are obtained. This is a **pre-launch blocker** — the platform cannot legally collect and remit sales/occupancy tax on vacation rental bookings without proper business registration.

## Current State

- **Stripe account:** Personal account (not business)
- **Business entity:** Not yet formed
- **Tax registrations:** None
- **Stripe Tax status:** Not activated (setup partially started — head office address set, but no tax registrations)
- **Code status:** ✅ Already implemented and forward-compatible
  - `automatic_tax: { enabled: true }` in `create-booking-checkout` edge function
  - Tax codes applied to line items (`txcd_99999999` for lodging, `txcd_10000000` for service fees)
  - `verify-booking-payment` extracts and stores tax amounts from Stripe
  - Fee breakdown UI shows "Taxes: Calculated at payment" (gracefully handles $0)
  - Migration 023 added tax fields to bookings table (`tax_amount`, `tax_rate`, `tax_jurisdiction`, `stripe_tax_calculation_id`)

## Steps Required

### 1. Form Business Entity
- [ ] Choose entity type (LLC recommended for liability protection)
- [ ] Register in Florida (state of primary operation — Jacksonville, FL)
- [ ] Obtain EIN (Employer Identification Number) from the IRS
- [ ] Open business bank account

### 2. State Tax Registrations
- [ ] Register for **Florida sales tax permit** (FL Dept of Revenue)
- [ ] Research and register in other states where RAV has tax nexus (based on property locations)
- [ ] Determine if local occupancy/tourist development taxes apply per county

### 3. Stripe Account Setup
- [ ] Convert personal Stripe account to business account (or create new)
- [ ] Enter business details (EIN, legal entity name, business address)
- [ ] Complete Stripe identity verification for the business

### 4. Stripe Tax Activation
- [ ] Update preset tax category from "Electronically Supplied Services" to **"Accommodation/Lodging"**
- [ ] Add tax registrations for each state (enter state tax permit numbers)
- [ ] Click "Configure Tax" to start collecting
- [ ] Repeat setup in **both sandbox and live** Stripe environments

### 5. Testing
- [ ] Complete sandbox setup first
- [ ] Run a test booking end-to-end and verify tax amounts appear correctly
- [ ] Verify tax amounts are stored in bookings table
- [ ] Verify owner payouts exclude tax (tax goes to Stripe for remittance)

## What Works Today Without Activation

Everything functions correctly with tax = $0:
- Bookings, payments, fee breakdowns, refunds all work
- The code is fully forward-compatible — once Stripe Tax is activated, tax amounts will automatically flow through
- No code changes will be needed when this is activated

## Blocked By

Business formation decision — entity type, state of incorporation, timeline.

## Related Issues

- #60 — Fee breakdown display (✅ completed)
- #61 — Stripe Tax integration code (✅ completed)

comments.body:
## Session 54 update — dev unblocked via env flag

The `create-booking-checkout` edge function was hard-coded with `automatic_tax: { enabled: true }`, which made booking flows fail in environments where Stripe Tax isn't activated (both DEV sandbox and pre-LLC PROD). Today's dev testing surfaced this with the error:

> `You must have a valid head office address to enable automatic tax calculation in test mode.`

### Fix

Gated `automatic_tax` behind a new `STRIPE_TAX_ENABLED` env var:

- **DEV + PROD (current state):** env var unset → `automatic_tax: { enabled: false }` → checkout completes, no tax calculated
- **PROD (post-#127):** set `STRIPE_TAX_ENABLED=true` to turn automatic tax back on

### When to flip the flag on PROD

Only after **all** of step 4 of this issue is complete:

- [ ] Business entity formed and verified in Stripe live account
- [ ] Head office / origin address entered on live Stripe Tax settings
- [ ] At least one tax registration added (FL minimum)
- [ ] Tax preset category set to **Accommodation/Lodging** (`txcd_20030000`)

Then run:
```bash
npx supabase secrets set STRIPE_TAX_ENABLED=true --project-ref xzfllqndrlmhclqfybew
```

And verify via a test booking that `tax_amount` appears in the `bookings` row.

### Related

- Deployed: `create-booking-checkout` to DEV (Supabase project `oukbxqnlxnkainnligfz`)
- Docs updated: `docs/LAUNCH-READINESS.md` rows 7b + Blocked Items
- Follow-up issue: #371 (add edge function test harness — shipped without tests today)
