## Summary

Build a `/blog` section on the website for SEO-driven content marketing. Educational content about timeshare clubs, destination guides, and "how to save on resort stays" drives organic search traffic and builds domain authority.

## Why a blog matters for RAV
- **SEO:** Long-tail keywords like "how to rent a timeshare week" or "Hilton Grand Vacations maintenance fees 2026" drive organic traffic from people who are our exact target audience
- **Authority:** Educational content positions RAV as the expert in timeshare rentals
- **Conversion funnel:** Blog → Browse listings → Book (top-of-funnel acquisition)
- **Backlinks:** Quality content attracts links from travel sites, personal finance blogs, timeshare forums

## Technical approach
- CMS decision pending (DEC-004) — options: MDX files in repo, headless CMS (Sanity/Contentful), or Supabase-powered
- Route: `/blog` (index) + `/blog/:slug` (individual posts)
- SEO: per-post canonical URLs, OG images, Article JSON-LD schema, author metadata
- Related to #229 (technical SEO — dynamic sitemap should include blog posts)

## Content themes (initial 10-15 articles)

### For timeshare owners (acquisition)
- "What Are Timeshare Maintenance Fees and Why Do They Keep Rising?"
- "5 Ways to Offset Your Timeshare Maintenance Fees"
- "How to Rent Out Your Timeshare Week (Step-by-Step Guide)"
- "Is Your Timeshare Week Sitting Unused? Here's What It's Costing You"
- "RAV SmartEarn: Calculate Your Timeshare's True Earning Potential"

### For travelers (acquisition)
- "How to Stay at a Hilton Grand Vacations Resort for 70% Less"
- "Best Orlando Timeshare Rentals for Families (2026 Guide)"
- "Timeshare Rentals vs. Hotels: A Real Cost Comparison"
- "Top 10 Marriott Vacation Club Resorts You Can Rent This Year"
- "How Voice Search is Changing Vacation Planning"

### For SEO / evergreen
- "What is a Timeshare Rental Marketplace?"
- "Timeshare Rental Scams: How to Spot Them and Stay Safe"
- "Guide to Disney Vacation Club Rentals"
- "[Destination] Vacation Guide" (one per top destination — Orlando, Myrtle Beach, Maui, etc.)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `/blog` route renders an index of published posts
- [ ] `/blog/:slug` renders individual posts with proper SEO (canonical, OG, Article schema)
- [ ] Blog posts included in dynamic sitemap
- [ ] At least 5 articles published at launch
- [ ] Mobile-responsive, accessible, fast (Core Web Vitals pass)

## Assignee scope
Two roles needed:
1. **Developer:** Build the blog infrastructure (route, CMS integration, SEO markup)
2. **Content writer:** Draft and edit articles (can be any founder or outsourced)

## Dependencies
- DEC-004 (CMS decision — not yet made)
- #229 (technical SEO — dynamic sitemap)
- Brand voice defined in `docs/brand-assets/BRAND-STYLE-GUIDE.md`

## Related issues
- #213 — Social media presence (blog posts = social content)
- #214 — Content strategy & campaign planning
- #229 — Technical SEO gaps

**Reviewed: April 17, 2026 — Sujit**

Decision: Apple Sign-In and Facebook OAuth will be implemented **only when we start the mobile app epic (#240)**. 

Rationale:
- Apple Sign-In is mandatory for App Store submission if any social login is offered — so it's a prerequisite for #248 (App Store submission), not a standalone feature
- Facebook OAuth targets the travel demographic but isn't needed until the platform has real users
- Supabase supports both providers natively — the build is straightforward (enable provider + add credentials)
- No value in adding these login options before the mobile app since Google OAuth already works for web

**Dependency chain:** This issue blocks #248 (App Store submission) and should be picked up as part of Phase 12 (Mobile App).

body:
## Accounting Integration — Pluggable Provider Architecture

**Primary provider:** Puzzle.io (native Stripe integration, free tier <$20K/mo)
**Architecture:** Provider-agnostic adapter pattern — swap to QuickBooks, Xero, or Zoho Books via configuration

### Why Puzzle.io over QuickBooks

| Criteria | Puzzle.io | QuickBooks Online |
|----------|-----------|-------------------|
| **Stripe integration** | Native API — 98% auto-categorization | Via Synder/Zapier middleware |
| **Free tier** | Yes — <$20K/mo transactions | No — $30-200/mo |
| **Revenue recognition** | Automated (ASC 606) | Manual |
| **Marketplace fit** | High — designed for platforms | Low — simple business focus |
| **API** | Modern REST | OAuth2 REST (more complex) |
| **Multi-user** | Unlimited (all plans) | Limited by tier |

### What the Accounting Tool Handles (vs. what RAV already does)

RAV already tracks all operational financial data in Supabase. The accounting tool provides:
- ✅ **General ledger** — official books (P&L, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow)
- ✅ **Bank reconciliation** — match Stripe payouts to bank deposits
- ✅ **Revenue recognition** — distinguish collected vs. earned revenue (ASC 606)
- ✅ **Expense tracking** — hosting, SaaS, domain costs (non-Stripe)
- ✅ **CPA-ready tax package** — annual business tax filing data

NOT needed from accounting tool (RAV handles natively):
- ❌ Fee calculation — `src/lib/pricing.ts`
- ❌ Owner payout orchestration — Stripe Connect + AdminPayouts
- ❌ Tax collection — Stripe Tax (code ready, pending activation)
- ❌ 1099-K generation — Stripe Connect handles natively ($2.99/form)
- ❌ Admin dashboards — AdminFinancials + AdminTaxReporting

### Implementation Plan

#### Phase 1: No-Code Setup (0 hours — manual)
- [ ] Create Puzzle.io account (free tier)
- [ ] Connect Stripe account to Puzzle.io (one-click)
- [ ] Verify transaction auto-sync working
- [ ] Set up chart of accounts (Revenue, Owner Payouts, Tax Liability, Expenses)

#### Phase 2: Pluggable Provider Architecture (4-6 hours)
- [ ] Create `src/lib/accounting/` adapter layer
  - `types.ts` — `AccountingEvent`, `AccountingProvider` interface
  - `puzzle-provider.ts` — Puzzle.io REST API implementation
  - `index.ts` — Factory that reads provider from `system_settings`
- [ ] `supabase/functions/sync-accounting/` edge function
  - Triggered by booking events (confirmed, payout, refund)
  - Reads provider config from `system_settings`
  - Sends standardized `AccountingEvent` to configured provider
- [ ] Admin UI: Accounting Integration settings panel
  - Provider selector (Puzzle.io / QuickBooks / Xero / Manual)
  - API key/credentials storage (encrypted)
  - Sync status dashboard (last sync, errors)
  - Manual sync trigger

#### Phase 3: Additional Providers (2-3 hours each, as needed)
- [ ] `quickbooks-provider.ts` — QuickBooks Online API
- [ ] `xero-provider.ts` — Xero API
- [ ] `zoho-provider.ts` — Zoho Books API

### Accounting Event Types

```typescript
type AccountingEvent =
  | { type: 'booking_confirmed'; booking: BookingFinancials }
  | { type: 'owner_payout'; booking: BookingFinancials; transfer: StripeTransfer }
  | { type: 'refund_processed'; booking: BookingFinancials; refundAmount: number }
  | { type: 'tax_collected'; booking: BookingFinancials; taxDetails: TaxBreakdown }
```

### Alternatives Evaluated

| Tool | Free Tier | Stripe | Marketplace Fit | Decision |
|------|-----------|--------|-----------------|----------|
| **Puzzle.io** | <$20K/mo | Native | High | **Selected** |
| Wave | Yes (limited) | Zapier only | Low | Too basic |
| Zoho Books | <$50K/yr | Basic | Medium | Runner-up |
| Xero | No ($20/mo) | App marketplace | Medium-High | Future option |
| QuickBooks | No ($30/mo) | Via middleware | Low | Legacy option |

### Key Insight: 1099-K Handled by Stripe Connect

Stripe Connect natively generates, e-files, and delivers 1099-K forms to owners.
- Auto-generates from transaction data
- E-files with IRS and states
- Delivers to connected accounts
- Cost: ~$2.99/form IRS, $1.49/form state
- No accounting software needed for this

### Reference
- DEC-022 (updated to reflect Puzzle.io decision)
- Docs: `docs/RAV-PRICING-TAXES-ACCOUNTING.md`

**Est. Time:** Phase 1: 0h (manual), Phase 2: 4-6h, Phase 3: 2-3h per provider
